folio.label: 132v
folio.l: Preye hire she go , no fastere cours than ©e
I seye , preietú ©oure Suster that she goo
No fastere cours than ©e , theise ©eres twoo
Thanne shal she ben euene , at the fulle alwey
And spryngö flood lastynge , bothe nygút and day
And but she vouche saf , in swicú manere
To graunte me , my souereigne lady deere
Preye hire to synken , euäy Rokke a doun
In to hire owen derk , regiou~
Vnder the ground , there Pluto dwelletú Inne
Or neuäe moo shal I , my lady wynne
Thy temple in delphos , wol I barfot seke
Lord Phebus , se the teeres on my cheke
And of my peyne , haue som compassiou~
And witú that word , in sorwe he fel a doun~
And longe tyme he lay fortú , in a traunce
¢ His brother whicú that knew , of his penaunce
Vp caugúte hi~ , and to bedde he hatú hi~ brougút
Dispeired in this tåment , and in in this thougút
Late I this wooful creature , lye
Chese he for me , wheiæä he wol lyue or dye
¢ Arueragus witú hele , and gret honour
As he that was , of Chyualrie the flour
Is comen hom , and othere worthy men
O bliful artow now , thow Dorigen
That hast thy lusty housbonde , in thyne armes
The fresshe knygút , the worthy man of armes
That louetú the , as his owne hertes lyf
No thyngö list hi~ , to ben Ymagintyf
If any wygút had spoke , whil he was oute
To hire of loue , he ne hadde of it , no doute
He nougút entendetú , to no swicú matere
But dauncetú Iustetú , maketú hire good chere
And thus in ioye and blisse , I late hem dwelle
And of the seke Aurelius , wol I telle
¢ In langour , and in turment furious
Two ©ere and more , lay wrecched Aurelius
Er any foot , he mygút on erthe goon
Ne comfort in this tyme , had he noon
Saue of his brother , whicú that was a clerk
He knew of al this woo , and al this werk
For to noon other creature , certeyn
Of this matere , he durst no word seyn
Vnder his brest , he bare it more Secree
Than euäe dide Pamphilus , for Galathee
His brest was hole , witú oute for to sene
